context.description: CSS sticky positioning implementation replacing JS-driven sticky behavior
changes: Added intermediate wrapper div.tabs-item-body between <details> and sticky content
Removed ~45 lines of JavaScript sticky code (updateStickyPanel, clearStickyTransforms, onScroll)
focusAreas: CSS sticky behavior on desktop with tall panels

build.details: Build completed successfully with webpack warnings (asset size limits - not critical)

blockInstances.details: Test page contains 9 tabs block instances with various configurations (2-8 tabs per instance)

cssStickyBehavior.details: CSS position:sticky working correctly on desktop. Intermediate wrapper div.tabs-item-body allows sticky positioning on div.tabs-item-inner. Content stays at top of viewport when scrolling through tall panels.

tabSwitching.details: Tab switching works correctly on desktop. Clicking different tabs updates active state, shows/hides appropriate panels, and sticky positioning continues to work.

mobileBehavior.details: Mobile accordion behavior works correctly. Details/summary elements toggle open/close on click. Tablist hidden on mobile viewports.

detailsBackground.details: Details element background (accent-lighter) stretches full height on desktop as expected. The md:open:flex md:open:flex-col md:open:flex-1 classes ensure the details element takes full height, and md:open:bg-accent-lighter applies the background color.

keyboardNavigation.details: Keyboard navigation works correctly using vertical tablist pattern (ArrowDown/ArrowUp instead of ArrowLeft/ArrowRight). Home/End keys work. Enter/Space activate tabs.
keyboardNavigation.note: The WAI-ARIA a11y test failed keyboard checks because it tests for horizontal tablist (ArrowLeft/ArrowRight), but this implementation uses vertical orientation (ArrowDown/ArrowUp) which is correct per aria-orientation='vertical' attribute.

ariaAttributes.details: All ARIA attributes present and correct: role='tablist' with aria-label and aria-orientation='vertical', role='tab' with aria-selected and aria-controls, role='tabpanel' with aria-labelledby. All ID references are valid.

patternCompliance.note: Keyboard checks failed in automated test because test expects horizontal tablist (ArrowLeft/ArrowRight) but implementation uses vertical tablist (ArrowDown/ArrowUp) which is correct per aria-orientation attribute

consoleErrors.details: No JavaScript errors detected. Screenshots captured successfully without console errors.

structureSync.details: Intermediate wrapper div.tabs-item-body and div.tabs-item-inner present in all block instances. Sticky classes correctly applied to inner wrapper.

issues.description: The taktician_a11y_test automated test flags keyboard navigation failures because it tests for horizontal tablist pattern (ArrowLeft/ArrowRight), but this implementation uses vertical tablist (ArrowDown/ArrowUp) which is the correct pattern given aria-orientation='vertical'.
issues.impact: None - this is a false positive in the automated test. The implementation is correct.
issues.recommendation: Update the accessibility test rules to check aria-orientation attribute and test the appropriate keyboard pattern (vertical vs horizontal) accordingly.
issues.fix: No fix needed for block implementation. The a11y test tool should be updated to handle vertical tablists.

passedChecks: CSS sticky positioning works on desktop
Sticky positioning persists when switching tabs
Intermediate wrapper div.tabs-item-body enables sticky on div.tabs-item-inner
Details element background stretches full height on desktop
All ARIA attributes present and correct
No sticky-positioning JavaScript code present (successfully removed)
recommendations: Consider updating the taktician_a11y_test tool to detect aria-orientation attribute and test the appropriate keyboard navigation pattern (vertical uses ArrowDown/ArrowUp, horizontal uses ArrowLeft/ArrowRight)
